name: The Old Warden Post
descriptionSeed: A shoulder of ground above the hollow, holding weather off the valley below. The post itself is ONE long room — stone-floored, low-beamed, a cold hearth on the east wall, two narrow windows facing the slope. A narrow alcove opens off the back with a stone shelf at waist height and a second small window. Just inside the door, a bench and a peg-rail, with a sight-line out over the hollow's mouth. Where the rafters meet, an apex stone. Built to watch a road nobody watches any more.
substrateNote: UNREACHED ground, not released — the third case in threeGrounds. The lattice here is thin because the old works never came this far out, not because anything was given up. Nothing was lost here; nothing arrived. Wards and craft laid in the hollow meet no competition and hold longer than they should.
worldPosNote: Valley shoulder-ground above the river settlements, a little out from Millbrook. Near-centre folk country. (SNG-180: authored position — WHERE it sits, independent of axisVector.)

the_long_room.name: The Long Room
the_long_room.note: The post, entire. Stone floor, low beams, a hearth gone cold on the east wall, two narrow windows onto the slope. Their light crosses at the centre, under the ridgeline.

the_south_corner.name: The South Corner
the_south_corner.note: Where the west window's light falls longest. Stone floor, good draw to the chimney, and a wall that will take a bench.

the_back_alcove.name: The Back Alcove
the_back_alcove.note: Narrow, off the main room. A stone shelf at waist height and a second small window. Room enough to receive one student at a time.

the_threshold.name: The Threshold
the_threshold.note: A narrow bench just inside the door, a peg-rail above it, and a sight-line straight out over the hollow's mouth. Whoever sits here holds who enters.

the_apex_stone.name: The Apex Stone
the_apex_stone.note: Where the rafters meet. The highest beam in the building, and the only place in it that sees the door without being near it.

claimableNote: These five are ARCHITECTURE — true for anyone who walks up that hill: a corner where the light falls longest and the chimney draws, an alcove that fits one student, a bench that holds the door, an apex that sees everything. What a company MAKES of them is that company's own and belongs in place-memory, never in this file. One company's forge corner is another's woodpile. A place is world content; what it becomes is a save.
encounterFlavor: Weather, and the sounds a building makes when it is deciding whether to keep standing. Scavengers who assumed it was empty. Someone from the settlements below come up to see who has moved in and why. Old warden fittings worth more as iron than as history. Nothing here hunts you — but nothing here is watching the road for you either, until someone starts.
questSeeds: The Post has no owner anyone can name, which is either an opportunity or a question somebody below has already asked
The corner tower's sightlines cover the road to the treeline — whoever holds it sees who comes to Stillwater first
The hollow behind the Post is thin ground of the unreached kind, and thin ground is worth something to the wrong people too
wardNote: SNG-180 / threeGrounds, confirmed in play 2026-07-19: the thin ground here is UNREACHED, and thin ground cuts both ways. Lattice-leaning craft pays MORE — shadow-work shapes cost above their rate here and a second one frayed before it closed. But what is laid down meets no competition and holds. The wards at this post are felt, not sealed: a deterrent, not a lock.
